raw_consumer_statement:
Subject : Appeal of Denied Fraud Dispute Unauthorized Recurring Charges $ [ XXXX ] Description : I am filing this complaint against Citibank regarding their failure to properly investigate and resolve a series of unauthorized charges on my XXXX XXXX XXXX card totaling {$1100.00}. 

Despite filing XXXX formal disputes, Citibank has denied my claims. Their stated reason is that " authorized account information '' was provided to the merchants. This conclusion is logically and legally flawed. While the card credentials ( authentication ) may have been used, I never authorized these transactions, nor do I have any relationship with these merchants. 

Key Points of Contention : No Prior Relationship : I have never purchased goods or services from these merchants. These are not " forgotten '' subscriptions ; they are fraudulent account takeovers or subscription scams. 

Lack of Evidence : Citibank has failed to provide the " documentary evidence '' they used to deny my claim, which I am entitled to under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( XXXX ). They have not produced a signed contract, an IP address log matching my location, or proof of delivery to my home address. 

Authentication vs. Authorization : Citibank is conflating the possession of card data with my personal authorization. Under the FCBA and Citibank 's own XXXX XXXX policy, my liability for unauthorized use should be {$0.00}. 

Payment Made Under Protest : I have already paid the disputed balance of {$1100.00} in full. This payment was made under protest solely to protect my credit score from negative reporting while this investigation continues ; it is not an admission of liability. 

Citibanks refusal to reverse these charges places the burden of merchant fraud entirely on the consumer. I request that Citibank reopen this case, conduct a thorough investigation into the merchants authorization logs, and issue a full credit to my account.
